Issues.Title: fix(docs-infra): exempt form validator names from API auto-linking
Issues.Body:
Add required, pattern, min, max, minLength and maxLength to LINK_EXEMPT so FieldState property names stop auto-linking to the validator functions of the same name.

fixes: #68479 

## PR Checklist

Please check if your PR fulfills the following requirements:

- [x] The commit message follows our guidelines: https://github.com/angular/angular/blob/main/contributing-docs/commit-message-guidelines.md
- [ ] Tests for the changes have been added (for bug fixes / features)
- [ ] Docs have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)


## PR Type

What kind of change does this PR introduce?

- [x] Bugfix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Code style update (formatting, local variables)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional changes, no api changes)
- [ ] Build related changes
- [ ] CI related changes
- [ ] Documentation content changes
- [ ] angular.io application / infrastructure changes
- [ ] Other... Please describe:


## What is the current behavior?

On `FieldState` (and similar) API pages, clicking property names like `required` and `pattern` navigates to the unrelated `required()` / `pattern()` validator functions instead of the property's anchor on the same page. The shiki transformer auto-links any identifier matching a known API symbol, so member names get hijacked when they share a name with an exported function.

Issue Number: #68479 


## What is the new behavior?

Adds `required`, `pattern`, `min`, `max`, `minLength`, `maxLength` to the existing `LINK_EXEMPT` set in `adev/shared-docs/pipeline/shared/linking.mts`, consistent with how `disabled`, `readonly`, `hidden`, `style`, etc. are already handled.


## Does this PR introduce a breaking change?

- [ ] Yes
- [x] No

Issues.Title: fix(docs-infra): preserve text fragments on docs page load
Issues.Body:
## PR Checklist

Please check if your PR fulfills the following requirements:

- [x] The commit message follows our guidelines: https://github.com/angular/angular/blob/main/contributing-docs/commit-message-guidelines.md
- [x] Tests for the changes have been added (for bug fixes / features)
- [ ] Docs have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)

## PR Type

What kind of change does this PR introduce?

- [x] Bugfix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Code style update (formatting, local variables)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional changes, no api changes)
- [ ] Build related changes
- [ ] CI related changes
- [ ] Documentation content changes
- [x] angular.dev application / infrastructure changes
- [ ] Other... Please describe:

## What is the current behavior?

Issue Number: #65119

Chrome's "Copy link to highlight" feature creates URLs with text fragments, for example:

```text
/guide/signals#:~:text=signal%20is%20a-,wrapper,-around%20a%20value
```

On Angular Docs, the native browser highlight appears briefly, but then disappears after the docs app finishes loading. The text fragment is removed from the URL because the app performs URL replacement during initial navigation.

## What is the new behavior?

Angular Docs now preserves browser text fragments during the initial same-path URL replacement.

When the current page was loaded from a text-fragment URL, the custom docs Location implementation updates history.state without passing a replacement URL to the History API. This keeps the browser-owned text fragment intact while still preserving Angular Router state updates and URL change notifications.

The fix also guards against stale navigation performance entries after SPA navigation, while still supporting text fragments combined with an element fragment prefix such as:

```text
/guide/signals#some-heading:~:text=signal%20is%20a
```
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open PR "fix(docs-infra): preserve text fragments on docs page load" (#68491)

Issues.Title: fix(forms): prevent native clamping on range inputs by applying bound…
Issues.Body:
When an input field receives a value that exceeds its bounds (like a `<input type="range">` where the default `max` is 100), the browser will natively clamp the value to fit within the valid range bounds.

Previously, `[formField]` set the native input `.value` before it configured `.max` and `.min` native properties. This meant any initial value above 100 for a range input would get clamped down to 100 before the upper bound was raised.

This commit fixes this issue by switching the order of execution in `control_native.ts`, ensuring bounds are configured on the native DOM node before the `controlValue` is applied.
Fixes #68480

Issues.Title: Signal formField range input with min/max does not correctly render
Issues.Body: ### Which @angular/* package(s) are the source of the bug? forms ### Is this a regression? No ### Description I want to display a `range` input with `min` & `max` limits. ### Using non-signal form control, I can do it this way: ```html {{ range.value }} ``` ```ts protected readonly range = new FormControl(150, { validators: [ Validators.min(100), Validators.max(200), ] }); ``` And it will correctly display the range input like this: ### But when switching to signal form fields: ```html {{ form.range().value() }} ``` ```ts protected readonly form = form(signal({ range: 150 }), (path) => { min(path.range, 100); max(path.range, 200); }); ``` It will not correctly display the range input: ### Please provide a link to a minimal reproduction of the bug _No response_ ### Please provide the exception or error you saw The **signal** range input sets the slider at the beginning instead of in the middle. ### Please provide the environment you discovered this bug in (run `ng version`) ```true Angular CLI : 21.2.8 Angular : 21.2.10 Node.js : 24.14.1 Package Manager : npm 11.13.0 Operating System : linux x64 ┌───────────────────────────┬───────────────────┬───────────────────┐ │ Package │ Installed Version │ Requested Version │ ├───────────────────────────┼───────────────────┼───────────────────┤ │ @angular/build │ 21.2.8 │ ^21.2.8 │ │ @angular/cli │ 21.2.8 │ ^21.2.8 │ │ @angular/common │ 21.2.10 │ ^21.2.10 │ │ @angular/compiler │ 21.2.10 │ ^21.2.10 │ │ @angular/compiler-cli │ 21.2.10 │ ^21.2.10 │ │ @angular/core │ 21.2.10 │ ^21.2.10 │ │ @angular/forms │ 21.2.10 │ ^21.2.10 │ │ @angular/platform-browser │ 21.2.10 │ ^21.2.10 │ │ @angular/router │ 21.2.10 │ ^21.2.10 │ │ rxjs │ 7.8.2 │ ~7.8.2 │ │ typescript │ 5.9.3 │ ~5.9.3 │ └───────────────────────────┴───────────────────┴───────────────────┘ ``` ### Anything else? For some reason, the problem doesn't happen with every min/max values, for instance, if I use `min=0, max=100 and value=50`, it works just fine, but with `min=100, max=200 and value=150` it's not working properly
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open issue "Signal formField range input with min/max does not correctly render" (#68480)

Issues.Title: Custom selects with <selectedcontent> and FormField crash the app with no error in an infinite loop
Issues.Body:
### Which @angular/* package(s) are the source of the bug?

forms

### Is this a regression?

No

### Description

Hello,

When trying to use the signals forms API with a custom select (using <selectedcontent>) the app will crash, probably stuck in an infinite re-rendering loop.

It works well without <selectedcontent>, so I supposed this has something to do with the way the content is projected by the standard API inside of the select when using this element.

### Please provide a link to a minimal reproduction of the bug

https://stackblitz.com/edit/stackblitz-starters-vbthyxw5?file=src%2Fapp%2Fapp.ts

### Please provide the exception or error you saw

```true
The app does not load at all, looping infinitely. I was not able to capture function calls to see what was causing this freeze.
```

### Please provide the environment you discovered this bug in (run `ng version`)

```true
Angular CLI       : 21.2.9
Angular           : 21.2.11
Node.js           : 20.19.5
Package Manager   : npm 10.8.2
Operating System  : linux x64

┌───────────────────────────┬───────────────────┬───────────────────┐

└───────────────────────────┴───────────────────┴───────────────────┘
```

### Anything else?

My intuition is that it might be related to [how selectedcontent works behind the scenes](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/selectedcontent#how_selectedcontent_works_behind_the_scenes), especially the cloneNode part, which I imagine may trigger some infinite rerendering loop.

Issues.Title: refactor-jasmine-vitest: add support for spy call arguments migration
Issues.Body:
### Command

generate

### Description

When running the `ng g @schematics/angular:refactor-jasmine-vitest` on a project which has the following line (for example, I have a few of those in my unit tests):
`expect(spy.calls.all()[2].args[0]).toBeInstanceOf(RemoveShareUrlAction);`
The migration does not remove `.args` and the code that was generated does not compile

### Describe the solution you'd like

In this specific case, the solution is simply to remove `.args` and the rest will work as expected.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

search and replace using the IDE

Issues.Title: feat(@angular/build): emit debug ids for stable subresource integrity hashes
Issues.Body:
Enable generating sub-resource integrity hashes when using error tracking tools relying on Debug IDs linking generated code files with source maps.

Closes #33108

## PR Checklist

Please check to confirm your PR fulfills the following requirements:

<!-- Please check all that apply using "x". -->

- [x] The commit message follows our guidelines: https://github.com/angular/angular-cli/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#-commit-message-guidelines
- [x] Tests for the changes have been added (for bug fixes / features)
- [ ] Docs have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)

## PR Type

What kind of change does this PR introduce?

<!-- Please check the one that applies to this PR using "x". -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Code style update (formatting, local variables)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional changes, no api changes)
- [ ] Build related changes
- [ ] CI related changes
- [ ] Documentation content changes
- [ ] Other... Please describe:

## What is the current behavior?

<!-- Please describe the current behavior that you are modifying, or link to a relevant issue. -->
When Angular generates source maps, they only include a link to the source map URL and no Debug ID:

```js
console.log(1);
//# sourceMappingURL=foo.js.map
```

The source map file also does not contain a debug ID:

```json
{ "version": 3, "debugId": "old", "mappings": "" }
```

Issue Number: #33108

## What is the new behavior?

When Angular generates source maps, in addition to the source map URL, generated files will also include a Debug ID above the `sourceMappingURL`:

```js
console.log(1);
//# debugId=11111111-2222-5333-9444-555555555555
//# sourceMappingURL=foo.js.map
```

In addition the source map file will include the matching Debug ID:

```json
{ "version": 3, "debugId": "old", "mappings": "", "debugId": "11111111-2222-5333-9444-555555555555" }
```

## Does this PR introduce a breaking change?

- [ ] Yes
- [x] No

<!-- If this PR contains a breaking change, please describe the impact and migration path for existing applications below. -->

## Other information

**I'm happy to hear if there are concerns with this particular approach and which alternative solutions might be a better solution.**

This allows error tracking to more reliably map generated code files to their corresponding stack traces.
Error tracking tools have historically solved this by running post-processing scripts that generate and inject Debug IDs after the framework build script and before deployment.

However, this approach will break any integrity hash that Angular generated for SRI as the file content will have changed.

-- 

This PR is part of a two fold effort to enable SRI for our specific integration of Angular.

The first PR, implementing support for dynamically loaded files can be found here, and should be merged first: #33109

Issues.Title: feat(@angular/build): subresource integrity validation for dynamically loaded modules
Issues.Body:
Adds support for verifying the integrity of dynamically loaded sub-resources by generating and pre-pending an import map with integrity hashes in the index.html

Closes #30724

## PR Checklist

Please check to confirm your PR fulfills the following requirements:

<!-- Please check all that apply using "x". -->

- [x] The commit message follows our guidelines: https://github.com/angular/angular-cli/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#-commit-message-guidelines
- [x] Tests for the changes have been added (for bug fixes / features)
- [ ] Docs have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)

## PR Type

What kind of change does this PR introduce?

<!-- Please check the one that applies to this PR using "x". -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Code style update (formatting, local variables)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional changes, no api changes)
- [ ] Build related changes
- [ ] CI related changes
- [ ] Documentation content changes
- [ ] Other... Please describe:

## What is the current behavior?

<!-- Please describe the current behavior that you are modifying, or link to a relevant issue. -->
Currently, no integrity checks are done for dynamically loaded files, such as chunks.

Issue Number: #30724

## What is the new behavior?

<!-- Please describe the new behavior that. -->
The integrity is validated by adding an importmap into as the first script of the `index.html` head with the integrity hashes of the dynamically loaded modules.

This will allow the browser to validate the integrity of dynamic imports.

The importmap will be added when Angular build is run with the `--subresource-integrity` flag.

## Does this PR introduce a breaking change?

- [ ] Yes
- [x] No

<!-- If this PR contains a breaking change, please describe the impact and migration path for existing applications below. -->

## Other information

This PR is part of a two fold effort to enable SRI for our use of Angular.

While this can be shipped on it's own, there is an additional issue blocking us from using SRI, due to Sentry relying on Debug IDs but Angular not outputting them automatically: #33108

I would appreciate a feedback on the following PR implementing Debug IDs in Angular sourcemaps: #33110

Issues.Title: Emit ECMA-426 source map debug IDs for stable subresource integrity
Issues.Body:
### Command

build

### Description

We are trying to implement Subresource Integrity checks while continuing to be able to track errors in our app reliably.

Error tracking tools, such as Sentry, have started to rely on Debug IDs to reliably associate source files with source maps.

The de-facto suggestion from those tools is to run a script to injecting the Debug IDs into the generated files and altering them after they have been built by e.g. Angular:
```
sentry-cli sourcemaps inject
```
[Sentry Docs](https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/javascript/sourcemaps/uploading/cli/#3-inject-debug-ids-into-artifacts)

However, altering the files breaks the integrity hashes of those files rendering the `--sri` option in the Angular unusable.

Related EMCA proposal: https://github.com/tc39/ecma426/blob/main/proposals/debug-id.md#debug-ids (Stage 2 as of 30th April, 2026)
Sentry documentation: https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/javascript/sourcemaps/troubleshooting_js/debug-ids/

### Describe the solution you'd like

Inject Debug IDs in a post-processing step after the output files and source maps have been generated and before the SRI hashes are generated by Angular.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

- Write a custom post-processing script implementing the calculation of subresource integrity hashes manually outside of the Angular build process.
  - → While this may work it's an annoying manual step that should automatically be done by Angular

- Let esbuild emit the Debug IDs in the source maps already.
  - → This is probably the cleanest solution 👀 Though requires the buy in from yet another team to adopt a stage 2 proposal and may delay until this natively lands in Angular 🥲
  - → The implementation of a post-processing step would be ~150 lines of code + some for tests.
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open issue "Emit ECMA-426 source map debug IDs for stable subresource integrity" (#33108)

### [`v4.4.1`](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/releases/tag/v4.4.1)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/compare/v4.4.0...v4.4.1)

#### Commits:

- [`481f7be`](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/commit/481f7be4238c83ed58183f921b2646f340a91c6a) ci: gate release publishing on full test workflow
- [`95ccab4`](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/commit/95ccab423aec720b2523c3a64cdc7e3204537cc7) test(v3): restore optional undefined expectations
- [`cede2c6`](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/commit/cede2c63739a5823d6aa5093d291e9a111da943d) fix(v4): reject tuple holes before required defaults ([#​5900](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5900))

### [`v4.4.0`](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/releases/tag/v4.4.0)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/compare/v4.3.6...v4.4.0)

#### 4.4.0

This is a minor release with a wide set of correctness and soundness fixes. Some fixes intentionally make Zod stricter, so code that depended on previously accepted invalid or ambiguous inputs may need small updates.

#### Potentially breaking bug fixes

##### Tuple defaults now materialize output values correctly

Fixed in [#​5661](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5661). Tuple parsing now more accurately reflects defaults, optional tails, explicit `undefined`, and under-filled inputs. The headline behavior is that defaults in tuple positions now properly appear in parsed output.

```ts
const schema = z.tuple([
  z.string(),
  z.string().default("fallback"),
]);

schema.parse(["a"]);
// ["a", "fallback"]
```

Trailing optional elements that are absent still stay absent; they are not filled with `undefined`.

```ts
const schema = z.tuple([
  z.string(),
  z.string().optional(),
]);

schema.parse(["a"]);
// ["a"]
```

But explicit `undefined` values supplied by the caller are preserved.

```ts
schema.parse(["a", undefined]);
// ["a", undefined]
```

When optional elements appear before later defaults, the parsed tuple is now dense so array operations behave predictably.

```ts
const schema = z.tuple([
  z.string(),
  z.string().optional(),
  z.string().default("fallback"),
]);

schema.parse(["a"]);
// ["a", undefined, "fallback"]
```

Tuple length errors are also more consistent now. Since `z.function()` arguments are tuple-shaped, function input errors may look different.

##### Required object properties with `z.undefined()`

Fixed in [#​5661](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5661), with follow-up coverage in [`57d80a82`](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/commit/57d80a82bde8877f3eb79e5dad9786096c37490f). A property whose schema is `z.undefined()` is now treated as required. The key must be present, but its value may be `undefined`.

```ts
const schema = z.object({
  value: z.undefined(),
});

schema.safeParse({}).success;
// false

schema.safeParse({ value: undefined }).success;
// true
```

Use `.optional()` when the key itself may be absent.

```ts
const schema = z.object({
  value: z.undefined().optional(),
});

schema.safeParse({}).success;
// true
```

This also affects related `.catch()`, `.partial()`, `.default()`, and `.prefault()` combinations that previously relied on missing `z.undefined()` keys being treated as optional.

##### Safer `.merge()` behavior with refinements

Fixed in [#​5856](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5856). The `.merge()` method now throws when the receiver has refinements, rather than silently producing ambiguous refinement behavior. Refinements from the second schema are preserved.

```ts
const a = z.object({ a: z.string() }).refine((val) => val.a.length > 0);
const b = z.object({ b: z.string() });

a.merge(b);
// throws
```

> Prefer `.extend()` or `.safeExtend()` for object composition. The `.merge()` method is still supported for compatibility, but it is discouraged for new code because its semantics around overlapping keys and refinements are easier to misread.

##### JSON Schema `$defs` entries no longer include redundant `id`

Fixed in [#​5759](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5759). JSON Schema conversion through `z.toJSONSchema()` now strips redundant `id` fields from `$defs` entries. This is required for correctness in older JSON Schema dialects from before `$id` was introduced: in those dialects, `id` changes the resolution scope, so leaving it inside an extracted definition can make references resolve incorrectly. The removed value was redundant because the schema had already been extracted into `$defs`, so the definition key itself is the identifier. This may affect consumers that were reading those internal `id` fields directly.

Other JSON Schema fixes in this release:

- Draft-04/OpenAPI 3.0 min/max intersections: [#​5700](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5700)
- Recursive lazy schemas with `.describe()`: [#​5797](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5797)
- Falsy prefault values emitted as defaults: [#​5893](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5893)
- CUID pattern output tightened: [#​5880](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5880)

##### String validators are stricter

Base64 validation now rejects whitespace instead of allowing `atob()`-style whitespace stripping. Fixed in [#​5888](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5888).

```ts
z.base64().safeParse("Zm9v").success;
// true

z.base64().safeParse("Zm 9v").success;
// false
```

Other string validator changes:

- CUID validation through `z.cuid()` has been tightened, and CUID v1 is now deprecated. Fixed in [#​5880](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5880).
- HTTP URL validation through `z.httpUrl()` now rejects malformed HTTP(S) URLs with a missing slash after the protocol. The underlying `URL` constructor normalizes inputs like `https:/example.com`, but Zod now rejects them instead of accepting the repaired URL. Fixed in [#​5672](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5672), related to [#​5284](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5284).

```ts
z.httpUrl().safeParse("https://example.com").success;
// true

z.httpUrl().safeParse("https:/example.com").success;
// false

z.httpUrl().safeParse("http:/www.apple.com").success;
// false
```

##### Union paths are fixed in formatted errors

Two union-related error fixes landed:

- Nested union paths are now preserved correctly in the output of `z.treeifyError()` and `z.formatError()`. Fixed in [#​5708](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5708) and [`60ff3987`](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/commit/60ff398771bb4b6df04d96f00b9a10ee561ee7af).
- Invalid discriminated union errors now include discriminator options and improved messages. Fixed in [#​5723](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5723). This may affect users snapshotting `ZodError` output.

#### Other fixes

##### Record key transforms now run

Fixed in [#​5891](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5891). Record schemas now run transforms on record keys.

```ts
const schema = z.record(
  z.string().transform((key) => key.toUpperCase()),
  z.number()
);

schema.parse({ foo: 1 });
// { FOO: 1 }
```

Related record fixes:

- Key refinement failures now surface as structured `invalid_key` issues. Fixed in [#​5719](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5719).
- Non-enumerable properties are skipped more consistently. Fixed in [#​5719](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5719).
- The v3-style single-argument `z.record(valueType)` form works again. Fixed in [`0e960108`](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/commit/0e960108f0d98dbea7844a8ff914f0980352f636).

##### Metadata and input handling in `fromJSONSchema()`

Schema generation from JSON Schema now applies metadata more consistently across `enum`, `const`, `not`, `anyOf`, and multi-type schemas. Fixed in [#​5758](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5758). It also rejects or normalizes more non-JSON-like inputs, including cyclic objects and `BigInt`. Fixed in [`87cf0f93`](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/commit/87cf0f93cd0f34bdc69f11c9377568e6812841c4).

##### Codecs

Codec changes:

- Encoding through `z.discriminatedUnion().encode()` now works when the discriminator uses a codec. Fixed in [#​5769](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5769).
- Codec inversion was added in [#​5770](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5770).

```ts
const stringToNumber = z.codec(
  z.string(),
  z.number(),
  {
    decode: Number,
    encode: String,
  }
);

const numberToString = stringToNumber.invert();
```

##### Transform context

Transform callbacks now support `ctx.addIssue()`. Fixed in [#​5699](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5699).

##### Conditional `.superRefine()` with `when`

The `when` option was added for `.superRefine()`. Added in [#​5741](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5741), with related abort behavior fixed in [#​5681](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5681).

##### Defaults for `Map` and `Set`

Defaults for `Map` and `Set` are now cloned instead of shared across parses. Fixed in [#​5855](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5855).

```ts
const schema = z.map(z.string(), z.number()).default(new Map());

const a = schema.parse(undefined);
const b = schema.parse(undefined);

a === b;
// false
```

##### Empty unions

Empty `z.union([])`, `z.xor([])`, and discriminated unions no longer crash at construction time. They construct and fail at parse time. Fixed in [#​5869](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5869).

##### Floating-point multiples

Number `multipleOf()` / `step()` validation is more accurate for decimal and exponent edge cases. Fixed in [#​5687](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5687) and [#​5793](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5793).

##### Global config and `jitless`

Configuration fixes:

- Global configuration is now shared through `globalThis`, improving behavior across mixed CJS/ESM module instances. Fixed in [#​5889](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5889).
- Jitless mode now avoids eval probing when set before first access. Fixed in [#​5864](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5864).

##### Prototype pollution hardening

Object catchall paths now skip `__proto__` keys. Fixed in [#​5898](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5898).

#### Performance improvements

##### Reduced memory usage from lazy-bound methods

Fixed in [#​5897](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5897). Classic builder methods are now lazy-bound through a shared internal prototype instead of eagerly attached per schema instance. This significantly reduces per-schema method allocation overhead, especially in codebases that construct many schemas. Detached methods continue to work:

```ts
const schema = z.string();
const optional = schema.optional;

optional.call(schema);
// still works
```

##### Improved tree-shaking

Implemented in [`195e8696`](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/commit/195e86962b5156012a4cdcfbff87dffddce87b78) and [#​5689](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5689). Top-level factory calls are annotated as pure, and generated stub package manifests now include `sideEffects: false`. This gives bundlers more room to remove unused Zod code.

This is intended as the conclusive fix for a long-standing class of tree-shaking and bundle-size issues, especially in Next.js and Turbopack projects. The most visible symptom was that unused validators and locales could survive bundling even when importing from `zod/mini` or from a narrow subpath.

Related reports include:

- Next.js and Turbopack tree-shaking reports: [#​4433](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/4433), [#​5641](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5641), [#​5095](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5095), [#​4810](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/4810)
- Locale and `zod/mini` bundle-size reports: [#​5561](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5561), [#​5665](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5665), [#​4369](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/4369), [#​4572](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/4572)
- Broader v4 bundle-size reports: [#​2596](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/2596), [#​4637](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/4637), [#​4798](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/4798), [#​5206](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5206)

```json
{
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

#### Locales

Added or updated locale support:

- Croatian: [#​5610](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5610)
- Greek: [#​5840](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5840)
- Romanian: [#​5657](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5657)
- Uzbek map support: [#​5599](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5599)
- Georgian translation fix: [#​5655](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5655)
- French issue origin translations: [#​5845](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5845)
- Italian validation message updates: [#​5852](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5852)

Locale message text changed in some cases, which may affect snapshots.

#### Closed issues

The following issues were closed by PRs included in this release:

- Closed [#​5466](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5466) via [#​5632](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5632): preserve context immutability in parse functions.
- Closed [#​5617](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5617) via [#​5655](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5655): correct Georgian translation for `string`.
- Closed [#​5619](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5619) via [#​5657](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5657): add Romanian locale.
- Closed [#​5229](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5229) via [#​5661](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5661): align object and tuple optionality handling.
- Closed [#​5680](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5680) via [#​5681](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5681): respect `abort: true` in `.refine()` checks with `when`.
- Closed [#​5678](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5678) via [#​5699](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5699): add missing `addIssue` to transform context.
- Closed [#​5717](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5717) via [#​5718](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5718): avoid `delete` in `finalizeIssue`.
- Closed [#​5714](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5714) via [#​5719](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5719): skip non-enumerable properties in record validation.
- Closed [#​5670](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5670) via [#​5723](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5723): add discriminator `options` to invalid discriminator errors.
- Closed [#​5743](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5743) via [#​5744](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5744): increase timeout for the datetime ReDoS checker test.
- Closed [#​5732](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5732) via [#​5758](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5758): apply description and default metadata in `fromJSONSchema()`.
- Closed [#​5731](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5731) via [#​5759](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5759): strip redundant `id` from `$defs` entries in JSON Schema output.
- Closed [#​5605](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5605) via [#​5763](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5763): update `z.custom()` docs for v4 compatibility.
- Closed [#​5593](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5593) via [#​5769](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5769): support `discriminatedUnion().encode()` with codec discriminators.
- Closed [#​5625](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5625) via [#​5770](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5770): add codec inversion.
- Closed [#​5778](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5778) via [#​5779](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5779): add custom docs 404 page.
- Closed [#​5792](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5792) via [#​5793](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5793): correct floating-point `multipleOf()` validation.
- Closed [#​5777](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5777) via [#​5797](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5797): resolve recursive lazy JSON Schema stack overflow.
- Closed [#​5805](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5805) via [#​5812](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5812): fix self-referencing schema docs.
- Closed [#​5826](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5826) via [#​5855](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5855): clone `Map` and `Set` defaults.
- Closed [#​5842](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5842) via [#​5856](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5856): align `.merge()` refinement semantics with `.extend()`.
- Closed [#​4461](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/4461) and [#​5414](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5414) via [#​5864](https://redirect.github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5864): honor `jitless` config in the eval probe.

Closes #33077

This PR contains the following updates: | Package | Change | [Age](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [Adoption](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [Passing](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [Confidence](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | [pnpm](https://pnpm.io) ([source](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/tree/HEAD/pnpm)) | [`10.33.0` → `10.33.2`](https://renovatebot.com/diffs/npm/pnpm/10.33.0/10.33.2) | ![age](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/npm/pnpm/10.33.2?slim=true) | ![adoption](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/adoption/npm/pnpm/10.33.2?slim=true) | ![passing](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/compatibility/npm/pnpm/10.33.0/10.33.2?slim=true) | ![confidence](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/npm/pnpm/10.33.0/10.33.2?slim=true) | --- - [ ] If you want to rebase/retry this PR, check this box --- ### Release Notes
pnpm/pnpm (pnpm) ### [`v10.33.2`](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/releases/tag/v10.33.2): pnpm 10.33.2 [Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/compare/v10.33.1...v10.33.2) ##### Patch Changes - Globally-installed bins no longer fail with `ERR_PNPM_NO_IMPORTER_MANIFEST_FOUND` when pnpm was installed via the standalone `@pnpm/exe` binary (e.g. `curl -fsSL https://get.pnpm.io/install.sh | sh -`) on a system without a separate Node.js installation. Previously, when `which('node')` failed during `pnpm add --global`, pnpm fell back to `process.execPath`, which in `@pnpm/exe` is the pnpm binary itself — and that path was baked into the generated bin shim, causing the shim to invoke pnpm instead of Node [#​11291](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/11291), [#​4645](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/4645). - Fix an infinite fork-bomb that could happen when pnpm was installed with one version (e.g. `npm install -g pnpm@A`) and run inside a project whose `package.json` selected a different pnpm version via the `packageManager` field (e.g. `pnpm@B`), while a `pnpm-workspace.yaml` also existed at the project root. The child's environment is now forced to `manage-package-manager-versions=false` (v10) and `pm-on-fail=ignore` (v11+), which disables the package-manager-version handling in whichever pnpm runs as the child. Fixes [#​11337](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/11337). ##### Platinum Sponsors
##### Gold Sponsors
### [`v10.33.1`](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/releases/tag/v10.33.1): pnpm 10.33.1 [Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/compare/v10.33.0...v10.33.1) ##### Patch Changes - When a project's `packageManager` field selects pnpm v11 or newer, commands that v10 would have passed through to npm (`version`, `login`, `logout`, `publish`, `unpublish`, `deprecate`, `dist-tag`, `docs`, `ping`, `search`, `star`, `stars`, `unstar`, `whoami`, etc.) are now handed over to the wanted pnpm, which implements them natively. Previously they silently shelled out to npm — making, for example, `pnpm version --help` print npm's help on a project with `packageManager: pnpm@11.0.0-rc.3` [#​11328](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/11328). ##### Platinum Sponsors
##### Gold Sponsors

Issues.Title: docs-bug(chips): reactive forms example has unsynced signal and FormControl (confusing source of truth)
Issues.Body:
### Is this a regression?

- [ ] Yes, this behavior used to work in the previous version

### The previous version in which this bug was not present was

_No response_

### Description

The "Chips inside of a Reactive form" example in the docs:
https://material.angular.dev/components/chips/overview#chips-reactive-form

maintains two separate pieces of state:

- A `signal` (`reactiveKeywords`) used to render the chips
- A `FormControl` bound to `<mat-chip-grid>`

However, these are not kept in sync:
- Adding/removing chips updates only the signal
- The FormControl value is never updated
- The UI reflects the signal, not `formControl.value`

This effectively creates two independent sources of truth.

This is confusing because reactive forms are typically based on a single source of truth (the FormControl), and the example does not explain why this divergence exists.


### Reproduction

StackBlitz link:  
https://stackblitz.com/edit/angular-material-chips-reactive-example

Steps to reproduce:
1. Open the example
2. Add or remove chips using the input
3. Inspect the FormControl value (e.g. log `formControl.value`)
4. Observe that it does not reflect the rendered chips

### Expected Behavior

The example should demonstrate a clear single source of truth.

Either:
- The chip list is derived from `formControl.value`, or
- The FormControl is kept in sync with the chip list

Alternatively, the docs should explicitly explain why two separate states are used.

### Actual Behavior

The UI is driven by a signal, while the FormControl is not updated.

This results in:
- Diverging state between UI and form
- Unclear guidance on how chips should be used with reactive forms in real applications

### Environment

- Angular: N/A (docs example)
- CDK/Material: latest (docs site)
- Browser(s): any
- Operating System: any

This PR contains the following updates: | Package | Change | [Age](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [Adoption](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [Passing](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [Confidence](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | [pnpm](https://pnpm.io) ([source](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/tree/HEAD/pnpm)) | [`10.33.0` → `10.33.2`](https://renovatebot.com/diffs/npm/pnpm/10.33.0/10.33.2) | ![age](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/npm/pnpm/10.33.2?slim=true) | ![adoption](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/adoption/npm/pnpm/10.33.2?slim=true) | ![passing](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/compatibility/npm/pnpm/10.33.0/10.33.2?slim=true) | ![confidence](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/npm/pnpm/10.33.0/10.33.2?slim=true) | --- - [ ] If you want to rebase/retry this PR, check this box --- ### Release Notes
pnpm/pnpm (pnpm) ### [`v10.33.2`](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/releases/tag/v10.33.2): pnpm 10.33.2 [Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/compare/v10.33.1...v10.33.2) ##### Patch Changes - Globally-installed bins no longer fail with `ERR_PNPM_NO_IMPORTER_MANIFEST_FOUND` when pnpm was installed via the standalone `@pnpm/exe` binary (e.g. `curl -fsSL https://get.pnpm.io/install.sh | sh -`) on a system without a separate Node.js installation. Previously, when `which('node')` failed during `pnpm add --global`, pnpm fell back to `process.execPath`, which in `@pnpm/exe` is the pnpm binary itself — and that path was baked into the generated bin shim, causing the shim to invoke pnpm instead of Node [#​11291](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/11291), [#​4645](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/4645). - Fix an infinite fork-bomb that could happen when pnpm was installed with one version (e.g. `npm install -g pnpm@A`) and run inside a project whose `package.json` selected a different pnpm version via the `packageManager` field (e.g. `pnpm@B`), while a `pnpm-workspace.yaml` also existed at the project root. The child's environment is now forced to `manage-package-manager-versions=false` (v10) and `pm-on-fail=ignore` (v11+), which disables the package-manager-version handling in whichever pnpm runs as the child. Fixes [#​11337](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/11337). ##### Platinum Sponsors
##### Gold Sponsors
### [`v10.33.1`](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/releases/tag/v10.33.1): pnpm 10.33.1 [Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/compare/v10.33.0...v10.33.1) ##### Patch Changes - When a project's `packageManager` field selects pnpm v11 or newer, commands that v10 would have passed through to npm (`version`, `login`, `logout`, `publish`, `unpublish`, `deprecate`, `dist-tag`, `docs`, `ping`, `search`, `star`, `stars`, `unstar`, `whoami`, etc.) are now handed over to the wanted pnpm, which implements them natively. Previously they silently shelled out to npm — making, for example, `pnpm version --help` print npm's help on a project with `packageManager: pnpm@11.0.0-rc.3` [#​11328](https://redirect.github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/11328). ##### Platinum Sponsors
##### Gold Sponsors

Issues.Title: fix(material/datepicker): use custom colors for date range comparison example
Issues.Body:
Fixes substandard colors used for the datepicker comparison ranges example since the migration to Material 3. Adds custom CSS variables to properly highlight in-range, overlap, and comparison states.

Fixes #31663
Issues.RelatedDescription: Closed or merged PR "fix(material/datepicker): use custom colors for date range comparison example" (#33158)

Issues.Title: docs(material/form-field): fix incorrect chip component reference
Issues.Body:
## Fix incorrect chips component in mat-form-field documentation

The documentation currently lists `mat-chip-set` as compatible with `mat-form-field`, but it does not implement `MatFormFieldControl` and results in the error:

> "mat-form-field must contain a MatFormFieldControl"

This PR updates the documentation to use `mat-chip-grid`, which correctly implements the required interface.

Additionally, a note was added to clarify that `mat-form-field` requires a component implementing `MatFormFieldControl`.

Closes #33035
Issues.RelatedDescription: Open PR "docs(material/form-field): fix incorrect chip component reference" (#33161)
